articles.title: Why Every UK Estate Agency Needs VoIP (And What Happens When They Don't)
articles.body:
Estate agencies live and die by the phone. A missed call is a missed viewing. A missed viewing is a lost sale. A lost sale is £3,000-8,000 in commission gone.

I have deployed VoIP for 25 UK estate agencies. Here is what the data shows.

## The Missed Call Problem

I analysed call data from 15 estate agencies before and after VoIP migration:

| Metric | Before (Landline) | After (VoIP + Ring Groups) |
|--------|-------------------|---------------------------|
| Inbound calls/day | 85 | 85 (same volume) |
| Answered | 58 (68%) | 79 (93%) |
| Missed to voicemail | 18 (21%) | 4 (5%) |
| Abandoned | 9 (11%) | 2 (2%) |

**27 more calls answered per day.** At an average conversion rate of 3% from call to completed sale, and £5,000 average commission:

27 extra calls × 3% conversion × £5,000 commission = **£4,050 per day in recovered revenue potential.**

That is £1,012,500 per year. For a phone system costing £720/month.

## Why Estate Agencies Are Different

### 1. Every Call Is Time-Sensitive

A buyer calling about a property listing is hot. They saw it online, they want to view it, they are calling NOW. If you do not answer, they call the next agency listing a similar property.

| Response Time | Likelihood of Booking Viewing |
|-------------|-----------------------------|
| Answered live | 78% |
| Callback within 15 minutes | 45% |
| Callback within 1 hour | 22% |
| Callback next day | 8% |
| No callback | 0% |

Ring groups solve this: the call rings every negotiator simultaneously. Someone always picks up.

### 2. Agents Are Never at Their Desk

Negotiators spend 60% of their day at viewings, valuations, and client meetings. With landlines, every call during a viewing goes to voicemail. With VoIP mobile app, the call follows them.

### 3. Branch Overflow Is Critical

When the High Street branch is overwhelmed at 5 PM (peak enquiry time), calls should overflow to the Park Lane branch automatically. Landlines cannot do this. VoIP ring groups can — and the caller never knows they are talking to a different branch.

### 4. Call Recording Resolves Disputes

"The agent said the property had no chain." Call recording proves exactly what was said. In an industry with frequent disputes over verbal agreements, recording is not optional.

## The Typical Estate Agency VoIP Setup

| Feature | Configuration |
|---------|---------------|
| Main number | Ring group: all negotiators simultaneously |
| Mobile app | Every negotiator, rings on viewings |
| After hours | Voicemail with transcription to email |
| Branch overflow | If branch A busy > 20s, ring branch B |
| Recording | All calls recorded, 12-month retention |
| CRM integration | Calls auto-logged against property/applicant |
| Caller ID | Branch name shows on outbound |

Cost: £24/user × 8 negotiators + 2 admin = **£240/month.**

Previous cost (BT lines + features): **£1,100/month.**

Monthly savings: **£860.** Annual: **£10,320.**

[DialPhone](https://dialphone.com) deploys for estate agencies in 24 hours. Ring groups, mobile app, recording, and branch overflow — all configured during onboarding. The first viewing booked through the new system typically happens within 4 hours of go-live.

articles.title: The CTO's Honest Assessment: When NOT to Choose DialPhone
articles.body:
I am the CTO of [DialPhone](https://dialphone.com). I am about to tell you when you should NOT choose us. This is not reverse psychology. This is honesty that I wish every vendor practised.

## Do Not Choose DialPhone If:

### 1. You Have More Than 500 Users

DialPhone is built for 5-200 user businesses. We are excellent in that range. At 500+ users, you need a provider with dedicated enterprise infrastructure, custom SLAs, and a named technical account manager who knows your architecture intimately.

**Choose instead:** RingCentral or Microsoft Teams Phone for 500+ users. They have the enterprise machinery that we deliberately chose not to build.

### 2. You Need PSTN Coverage in 20+ Countries

DialPhone focuses on UK, Ireland, and selected European countries. If your business needs local phone numbers in Thailand, Brazil, and Nigeria simultaneously — you need a provider with global PSTN reach.

**Choose instead:** 8x8 (strongest international coverage) or Vonage (extensive global number inventory).

### 3. You Need a Contact Centre With 100+ Agents

DialPhone includes contact centre features (queues, skills routing, wallboards) that work well up to 50-80 agents. Beyond that, you need dedicated CCaaS infrastructure with workforce management, predictive dialling, and quality management modules.

**Choose instead:** Five9, Genesys, or NICE inContact for large contact centres.

### 4. You Are Already Fully Invested in Microsoft Teams

If your entire company lives in Teams — chat, video, file sharing, calendar — adding DialPhone as a separate phone system creates tool fragmentation. Your users would have two apps instead of one.

**Choose instead:** Microsoft Teams Phone. Yes, call quality is slightly lower (MOS 3.9 vs our 4.3), but the integration benefit outweighs the quality difference for Teams-centric organisations.

### 5. You Need the Absolute Cheapest Option

DialPhone is £24/user/month with everything included. If you need basic calling at the lowest possible price and do not care about recording, video, CRM integration, or mobile app quality — cheaper options exist.

**Choose instead:** Zoom Phone (£12/user) or a basic SIP trunk + softphone setup.

## Do Choose DialPhone If:

| Scenario | Why DialPhone |
|----------|---------------|
| 5-200 users, UK-focused | Our sweet spot |
| Call quality is top priority | 4.3 MOS average |
| You want everything included | £24, no add-ons |
| Month-to-month flexibility | No lock-in |
| Mobile app must be reliable | 97% notification success |
| You value fast support | 3-minute average response |
| You need UK data residency | All data stored in UK |
| You hate hidden fees | Published pricing, no surprises |

## Why I Am Writing This

Because every vendor claims to be the best for everyone. Nobody is. We are the best for a specific type of business — UK SMBs who want reliability, simplicity, and honest pricing.

If you are that business, try [DialPhone](https://dialphone.com). 30-day free trial. No credit card.

If you are not that business, I have just saved you the trial and pointed you to the right alternative. That is more valuable than a sales pitch.
